title: nirs4all Dataset Fetch Manifest v2
$comment: The FETCHER contract for a nirs4all-datasets dataset (datasets/<id>/manifest.json, schema_version 2.0). This is the artifact an R/JS/WASM client ports a thin fetcher over: for each entry, resolve the bytes (by DOI/open-origin URL), stream to a local cache, and verify sha256 before use. `canonical_hashes` and `row_counts` let a client validate the materialized canonical Parquet without re-parsing. No Python is required to fetch+verify. Writes are local-cache only; there is no upload/publish field in this contract.

path.$comment: Relative path under the dataset dir, e.g. 'canonical/sources/X.parquet'.

role.$comment: raw|canonical. Open vocabulary; raw are origin bytes, canonical are the materialized Parquet a consumer loads.

sha256.$comment: Lowercase hex SHA-256 the fetcher MUST verify after download.

canonical_hashes.$comment: Map of canonical Parquet basename -> sha256, a fast-path integrity check for the materialized form.

row_counts.$comment: Map of canonical table name -> row count.

doi.$comment: DOI that pins remote bytes for fetch-by-DOI; null for local/open-origin-only datasets.
